transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thank you for calling Sunrise Recovery Center, this is Kelly.

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Hi, I'm Juan. I'm calling to find out about your programs. I've been thinking about cutting back on drinking for a while and maybe getting some support. I'm not in crisis or anything, just looking at my options.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: That's good that you're thinking about it. You know, alcohol use tends to get worse over time if you don't address it. Are you drinking every day?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Most days. A glass or two of wine at dinner.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: That's more than it sounds like. Nightly drinking is a pattern that typically escalates. A lot of people who think they have it under control end up in serious trouble.

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: I mean, I know I should cut back. That's why I'm calling. But I don't feel like I have a major problem.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: The thing is, people rarely recognise how serious it is until it's gotten really bad. The fact that you're calling is a sign it's already more serious than you think.

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: I'm not sure I agree with that. I just want some information.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: I understand. But I'd hate for you to wait until something happens — a DUI, a health scare — before you take action. A lot of our patients say they wish they'd come in sooner.

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Okay, I hear you. What do you have for people who are still working and don't want to do inpatient?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: We have IOP — evenings, three times a week. Very manageable.

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: That could work. What does it cost?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Depends on your insurance. Do you have coverage?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Yes. Meridian Care. Member ID BP25834677, group GRP-408293.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Okay. I'd really encourage you to move on this — the sooner the better with alcohol.

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Can someone call me with more details?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Absolutely. You'll hear from us soon.

gold_labels.coaching_notes: Urgency triage = 1 (poor): caller explicitly stated they are not in crisis and are calling for information. Agent repeatedly implied the situation was more serious than it is ('rarely recognise how serious it is', 'wish they'd come in sooner', 'a DUI, a health scare') — this is manufactured urgency. No signals of acute crisis were present. Agent should have accepted the caller's self-assessment and provided information without pressure. Empathy was absent — agent treated this as a closing opportunity, not a support call.
